label: Strategic Tensions
headline: Deployment automation is being bypassed instead of repaired
summary: Release and hotfix workflows keep falling back to manual coordination while architectural coupling remains unresolved.

tiles.title: Broken hotfix workflow
tiles.blurb: The intended hotfix path broke often enough that manual intervention became the default escape hatch.

tiles.title: Release process friction
tiles.blurb: Release coordination started absorbing architecture work because deployment boundaries stayed blurry.

tiles.title: Version drift between services
tiles.blurb: Hosted and OSS services drifted independently, so automation could no longer assume compatible artifacts.

tiles.title: Manual deploy playbook
tiles.blurb: The workaround became institutional knowledge, masking the fact that the root fix was still missing.

label: Quiet Assumptions
headline: Silent state transitions hide initialization failures until downstream work breaks
summary: Multiple setup flows appear complete before they are actually usable, which turns missing finalization into a delayed failure mode.

tiles.title: Attached functions lifecycle
tiles.blurb: The setup flow looks successful before the finalize step has actually made the function runnable.

tiles.title: Asynchronous validation design
tiles.blurb: Validation moved downstream, making correctness depend on later async work rather than the initial write succeeding cleanly.

tiles.title: Segment model architecture
tiles.blurb: Downstream systems operate as if state is valid, so failures surface later and farther from the original action.

tiles.title: Metadata database design
tiles.blurb: The metadata layer still carries unresolved questions about when state should be considered durable versus merely accepted.

label: Operational Pressure
headline: Multi-region rollout is moving faster than infrastructure readiness and decision clarity
summary: Expansion work keeps surfacing unresolved ownership, deployment, and reliability questions that were manageable in one region but compound across many.

tiles.title: Multi-region strategy
tiles.blurb: The strategic push is clear, but foundational decisions are still being worked out in parallel with rollout pressure.

tiles.title: Region failover requirements
tiles.blurb: Reliability requirements reveal gaps that were easy to postpone in the single-region setup.

tiles.title: Deployment topology tradeoffs
tiles.blurb: Topology choices now affect recovery posture, operator burden, and how quickly new regions can come online.

tiles.title: Customer expansion requests
tiles.blurb: Customer pull keeps compressing the time available to resolve the underlying infrastructure choices well.

label: Architecture Under Stress
headline: Fragmented task mapping creates silent search failures across systems
summary: Several systems rely on mappings that drift quietly, so search can look healthy while recall and routing degrade underneath it.

tiles.title: Task routing overview
tiles.blurb: Routing logic depends on mappings that are maintained across boundaries rather than enforced in one place.

tiles.title: Search indexing pipeline
tiles.blurb: Indexing and mapping assumptions live in separate layers, making drift easy to miss during normal operations.

tiles.title: Query debugging playbook
tiles.blurb: Operators compensate with manual debugging because failures often look like low relevance instead of outright errors.

tiles.title: Cross-system ownership gaps
tiles.blurb: No single boundary owns the mapping contract end to end, so the same issue can recur in new forms.
